message:
Bench dynamic vs codegen — dynamic wins on reads

Quantifying the dispatch cost of capnwasm/dynamic vs codegen-emitted
classes. Wired up as `npm run bench:dynamic`.

Surprise on the read side: dynamic is *faster* than codegen, ~0.7× on
read-all-13 and ~0.9× on pick-3. Both paths make the same number of
wasm-boundary calls (the wasm side does the heavy lifting, JS just
dispatches). The codegen reader is more polymorphic — distinct getter
per field, more hidden-class transitions; the dynamic reader's
_readSingle is one switch-on-type that V8 turns into a tight loop after
warmup.

Write side, 13 sets + finalize: codegen ~943 ns, dynamic ~1370 ns/call
(1.45× slower). Codegen bakes offsets as integer literals at the call
site; the dynamic builder hits a Map for each field name and a
switch-on-type for each set. For tenant-uploaded schemas where you
can't run codegen anyway, that's still 1.4 µs per built message —
plenty fast for any realistic RPC workload.

Numbers are on Node 22 with 50k iterations after a 200-iteration warm.
README cites them in the "Runtime-schema reader" section so people
choosing between codegen and dynamic see the cost upfront.

files.patch:
@@ -213,6 +213,16 @@ const bytes = b.finalize();   // framed Cap'n Proto bytes, wire-compatible with
 
 Builders cover primitives + text + data. Lists and nested-struct builders aren't in this pass — codegen still wins for those write paths.
 
+**How fast?** Bench on Node 22, conformance schema's 13-field Primitives struct, 50k iterations after warmup. The dynamic path is competitive on reads — it's effectively tied with codegen because both do the same wasm-boundary calls and the per-field dispatch is small relative to that. Writes are ~1.45× slower because codegen bakes offsets as integer literals at the call site while the dynamic builder dispatches via switch-on-type per field.
+
+```
+read all 13 fields           codegen ~745 ns,  dynamic ~511 ns/call
+read 3 fields (pick)         codegen ~459 ns,  dynamic ~413 ns/call
+build with 13 fields         codegen ~943 ns,  dynamic ~1370 ns/call
+```
+
+`npm run bench:dynamic` reproduces these.
+
 When to choose dynamic:
 
 - **You don't control the schemas at build time** (tenant-supplied schemas, admin tooling)
